basic_info.name: Ocean Flower Island Water Kingdom / Children's World Water Park (海花岛童世界水上王国)
basic_info.location: South side of Snow Mountain Ocean Zone, Island No. 1, Ocean Flower Island, Binhai New District, Danzhou City, Hainan Province, China (海南省儋州市滨海新区海花岛1号岛雪山海洋区南侧)
basic_info.operator: Hengda Children's World Group (恒大童世界集团) via Danzhou Changyu Tourism Development Co., Ltd. (儋州昌宇旅游开发有限公司); originally developed by Evergrande Group (恒大集团) on Ocean Flower Island master-plan
basic_info.opening_date: 2021-06-01 (initial trial opening); reopened 2023-03-24 after upgrade; relaunched again 2024-04-20 after second renovation

technical_specs.wave_technology_brand: Wave system from major international waterpark equipment supplier (park designed by WTI - WhiteWater Tube & Inflatables / WTI Design); slides and racer mats supplied by ProSlide, WhiteWater West, Dalang, and Guangdong H-Fun [uncertain on specific wave generator brand]
technical_specs.pool_size: Total water park area approximately 110,000 m² (some sources cite 130,000 m²); central wave pool 'Hurricane Wave Bay' (飓浪谷) approximately 6,000 m²
technical_specs.wave_height: Up to approximately 3.0 m at peak wave cycles in the Hurricane Wave Bay (described as China's largest indoor-style hurricane wave pool); typical recreational waves 0.3-1.5 m [uncertain on exact maximum]
technical_specs.wave_height_adjustability: Multi-mode wave generation cycling between calm, gentle rolling waves, and dramatic 'hurricane' surge waves on a programmed schedule; specific named tier levels not publicly published [uncertain]
technical_specs.wave_types: Recreational rolling waves and surge / hurricane waves for floating, bobbing, bodyboarding, and 'warrior surfing' (勇士冲浪) attraction; not designed for stand-up surfboard riding
technical_specs.waves_per_hour: Operates in cycles of active wave sets followed by rest intervals; specific waves-per-hour rate not publicly documented [uncertain]
technical_specs.wave_count_per_set: Set durations and per-set wave counts not publicly published; typical hurricane-mode cycles run several minutes of building swell followed by calm intervals [uncertain]
technical_specs.session_length: Continuous access during park operating hours; no per-session time limit on the wave pool [uncertain]
technical_specs.stance_rotation_schedule: N/A - not a surfing-focused pool; no Goofy/Regular rotation
technical_specs.wave_type_classification: Artificial recreational family wave pool with hurricane surge mode; snow-mountain themed; not a dedicated surf-shaped wave pool

pricing_sessions.price_range: Adult day ticket approximately RMB 208; child ticket approximately RMB 115; group / promotional pricing from RMB 50; combined Ocean Paradise + Water Kingdom family packages approximately RMB 577 for 2 adults + 1 child; night session (added in 2025) opens 18:00 [uncertain on current 2026 pricing]
pricing_sessions.session_skill_tiers: N/A - no skill-tiered surf sessions; single general admission grants access to wave pool and all 18 water attractions
pricing_sessions.equipment_rental: Life jackets typically provided free; lockers, towels, and swimwear available for rent on-site (typical RMB 30-80 deposits); the park does not provide free swimsuits and visitors must wear swimwear [uncertain on exact current fees]
pricing_sessions.coaching_fee: N/A - no surf coaching offered; the park is recreational family-oriented
pricing_sessions.membership_program: Annual passes and combo tickets bundling Ocean Paradise + Water Kingdom + on-island accommodation occasionally offered; specific Water-Kingdom-only annual pass pricing not publicly listed [uncertain]
pricing_sessions.corporate_team_building: Yes - the resort markets MICE, corporate retreats, and team-building packages via the world's largest convention center on the island; minimum group size and pricing handled case-by-case [uncertain on published rates]

experience_guide.best_season: Water Kingdom typically operates April-November with seasonal closures in winter; peak experience May-October when air and water temperatures are warmest; avoid Chinese national holidays (Spring Festival, Labor Day, National Day Golden Week, July-August summer break) for shorter queues
experience_guide.best_swell_window: N/A - artificial wave pool, not a natural surf spot
experience_guide.crowd_level: Generally moderate due to remote location on Danzhou's west coast; very high during Chinese public holidays and summer school break; relatively quiet on weekdays in shoulder season; user reviews note 'the queues are not very long' even during normal peak periods
experience_guide.booking_method: Online pre-purchase strongly recommended via OTAs; tickets also sold at the park gate; same-day reservation typically available
experience_guide.booking_platform: Ctrip (携程) / Trip.com, Meituan-Dianping (大众点评), Fliggy (飞猪), Qunar (去哪儿), Tuniu (途牛), Douyin (抖音), official Ocean Flower Island WeChat mini program
experience_guide.payment_options: Alipay, WeChat Pay, UnionPay, cash (RMB); international credit cards limited acceptance at park ticket counters but accepted at on-island international hotels [uncertain on current international card support at park gate]
experience_guide.skill_required: Zero - no surfing skill required; suitable for non-swimmers in shallow areas with life jackets and lifeguard supervision
experience_guide.age_eligibility: Children under 1.2 m height typically free or discounted; 1.2-1.5 m children's ticket; over 1.5 m adult ticket. Minors under 16 require adult supervision. Several thrill slides have minimum 1.2 m or 1.4 m height requirements; guests over 60 years or weighing over 90 kg are restricted from most rides [uncertain on exact wave-pool-specific limits]
experience_guide.child_friendly_score: 5 - explicitly designed as a snow-mountain themed family/children's water park with baby slides, zero-entry shallow areas, themed costumes, and dedicated kids' zones; co-located with the marine animal park makes a full family day out
experience_guide.sup_paddleboard_offering: Not offered as a structured program at the wave pool [uncertain]
experience_guide.photography_video_service: On-site professional photographers at major slides and wave pool; photo packages sold at exit kiosks; pricing varies seasonally [uncertain on exact current rates]
experience_guide.language_support: Mandarin primary; limited English support at ticket counters and rides; full multilingual service available at on-island international-brand hotels (some operating, others delayed) [uncertain on current staffing]
experience_guide.accessibility_for_disabled: General park pathways are wheelchair accessible; persons with valid disability ID receive discounted or free admission; no dedicated adaptive surfing program at the wave pool [uncertain on detailed wave-pool entry accessibility]
experience_guide.stance_preference_input: N/A - not a surf training pool
experience_guide.safety_notes: Lifeguards posted at all pools and water attractions per Chinese national waterpark safety standards; mandatory life jackets for non-swimmers and children; bag security screening at entry; pregnant women, those with heart conditions, recent surgery patients, intoxicated guests, and visitors over 60 or 90+ kg prohibited from most rides. Personal accident insurance bundled into ticket per Chinese theme park regulations
experience_guide.events_competitions_calendar: Hosts seasonal entertainment including 'Snow Mountain Night Show' (雪山夜秀) light performances added in 2025, Danzhou electronic music parties, water battles, and holiday-themed events; does not host competitive surfing events

surroundings.nearby_accommodation: Numerous Evergrande-developed apartment-style properties on Ocean Flower Island Islands 2 and 3, with rates RMB 400-1,500 per night via short-term-rental platforms; the broader Danzhou and Yangpu coastline have limited additional independent accommodation options, generally requiring 30-60 minute drive
surroundings.on_site_accommodation: Ocean Flower Island master-plan called for 58 hotels including a 7-star Peninsula-branded property and European-style castle hotel; many hotels were completed but operational status varies due to the Evergrande financial restructuring; currently operating on-island hotels include several mid-tier and apartment-style properties with rates from approximately RMB 500-2,500 per night [uncertain on specific operating hotel inventory in 2026]
surroundings.nearby_food: On-island: 12+ ocean-themed restaurants in the Children's World complex, 8 themed food streets, submarine-themed bar, and 6 commercial streets across the master plan (operational status of individual outlets varies); off-island: Yangpu Bay seafood villages and Baima Jing town (白马井镇) restaurants approximately 20-30 minutes drive
surroundings.transport: Self-drive (free island parking and bridge access from mainland), Didi/taxi from Danzhou, internal monorail and inter-island shuttle on Ocean Flower Island, hotel shuttle services from Haikou and Sanya offered by some on-island hotels
surroundings.transport_to_nearest_airport: Approximately 130 km / 1.5-2 hours drive from Haikou Meilan International Airport (HAK); approximately 250 km / 3 hours from Sanya Phoenix International Airport (SYX); nearest high-speed rail station is Baima Jing (白马井站) on the Hainan Western Ring High-Speed Railway, approximately 15 km / 20 minutes drive with airport/HSR shuttle services available
surroundings.combined_itinerary: Combine Ocean Flower Island Water Kingdom + Children's World Ocean Paradise + Snow Mountain Children's World theme park (multi-day pass); pair with Yangpu Bay mangrove ecological park, Dongpo Academy (东坡书院) Su Shi historical site in Danzhou, and Lan Yang Bay (兰洋温泉) hot springs; for the broader Hainan west coast loop, extend to Linchang (临场) salt fields and the Stone Flower Cave (石花水洞) geological park

pros: Massive 6,000 m² 'Hurricane Wave Bay' wave pool capable of generating ~3 m waves - one of the largest in Hainan
Unique snow-mountain themed design - the only such concept in South China
Co-located Children's World Ocean Paradise marine park makes a full multi-day family destination
WTI-designed with internationally award-winning slides from ProSlide and WhiteWater West
Generally less crowded than Atlantis Sanya with shorter queues even in peak periods
cons: Remote west-coast location far from Sanya tourism cluster - long drive or HSR transfer required
Operating status and visitor experience affected by ongoing Evergrande Group restructuring; some on-island hotels and amenities remain unfinished or non-operational
Seasonal water-park closures in winter months
Limited international F&B and hospitality services compared to Sanya destinations
Past controversy over illegal-construction demolition orders (2021-2022) and 'Ugliest Buildings in China' ranking has affected overall island reputation [uncertain on current resolution status]
reviews_reputation.user_reviews: Ctrip (携程) reviews praise the wave pool and unique snow-mountain theming, with multiple positive notes about minimal queues; Dianping (大众点评) reviews highlight the value-for-money family packages and friendly staff; Xiaohongshu (小红书) extensive coverage of the photogenic snow-mountain backdrop and ferris wheel; some reviews caution about heat exposure and the long travel distance from Haikou or Sanya [uncertain on exact current scores]
reviews_reputation.recommended_for: Families with children, road-trippers exploring Hainan's west coast, multi-generational travelers, photo/social-media visitors seeking unique snow-mountain backdrop, budget-conscious families avoiding Sanya pricing; NOT recommended for serious surfers seeking training waves or short-trip visitors based in Sanya
reviews_reputation.operator_brand_partnership: Originally developed by Evergrande Group (恒大集团) under its Children's World tourism division; current operator is Hengda Children's World Group via Danzhou Changyu Tourism Development Co., Ltd.; designed by WTI (WhiteWater Tube & Inflatables) Design and US firm IdeAttack (艾迪泰克) with PGAV; slides and equipment by ProSlide, WhiteWater West, Guangdong H-Fun, and Dalang
